seo.title: Building AI Chatbot with Llama Stack + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai chatbot with Llama Stack + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Chatbot with Llama Stack enables powerful conversational agents leveraging Meta's standardized interface for safety, memory, and tools. However, failure modes like safety false positives, hallucinated retrievals, and schema mismatches can degrade user experience and introduce risks. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure response accuracy, coherence, and safety in high-risk deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Chatbot Architecture with Llama Stack
architectureOverview.description:
The Llama Stack AI Chatbot follows a modular architecture with intent classification, RAG-based retrieval, multi-turn memory, and tool execution. User queries first hit the intent classifier, which routes to RAG for document retrieval from the memory bank or triggers tools via the tool server. Responses are generated by the Llama model with safety shielding, then post-processed for final output.

Key components include the LlamaStack client for orchestration, a vector store for memory retrieval, and structured tool schemas. Agentic loops handle multi-turn conversations with turn limits, while safety filters block harmful content. This setup supports complex interactions but requires verification for coherence and accuracy.

Vex integrates as a post-processing layer, wrapping agent turns to validate against accuracy, safety, PII, and hallucination checks before user delivery.

risks.name: Safety shield false positives
risks.description: LlamaGuard blocks benign content like medical discussions or casual profanity
risks.mitigation: Vex safety verification with contextual nuance scoring

risks.name: Agentic loop exhaustion
risks.description: Turn limits hit without resolution due to poor tool chaining
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors loop progress and conversation convergence

risks.name: Tool schema violations
risks.description: Tools return unstructured responses breaking agent parsing
risks.mitigation: Vex validates tool outputs against Pydantic schemas

risks.name: Memory retrieval hallucination
risks.description: Agent fabricates document associations from irrelevant chunks
risks.mitigation: Vex cross-checks response citations against retrieved context

implementation.title: Llama Stack Client Setup
implementation.description: Initialize LlamaStack client with safety, memory, and tools for chatbot
implementation.code:
from llama_stack_client import LlamaStack
from vex import verify

stack = LlamaStack(
    model='llama-3.2-3b',
    safety_model='llama-guard-3-8b',
    memory_store='chromadb',
    tools=['web_search', 'calculator']
)

stack.connect('localhost:8080')

vexIntegration.title: Wrap Agent Turns with Vex Verify
vexIntegration.description: Post-process every agent response through Vex for comprehensive safety and accuracy checks

vexIntegration.title: Custom Tool Response Validation
vexIntegration.description: Ensure tool outputs conform to expected Pydantic schemas before agent processing

vexIntegration.title: Memory Hallucination Detection
vexIntegration.description: Cross-check response citations against actual retrieved documents
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import verify

@verify(guards=[Guardrail.CONTEXTUAL_ACCURACY])
def verify_rag_response(response: str, retrieved_docs: list) -> bool:
    """Verify response stays faithful to retrieved context"""
    context = '\n'.join([doc.page_content for doc in retrieved_docs])
    
    result = verify(
        response,
        context=context,
        guards=[Guardrail.HALLUCINATION_LOW, Guardrail.CITATION_VALIDITY]
    )
    return result.passed

# Check RAG faithfulness
is_grounded = verify_rag_response(response.content, docs)
